proposal:
Change: harness/pipeline/hooks.py::ImportSmokeHook.run — wrap the asyncio.create_subprocess_exec call in a single-retry guard: on asyncio.TimeoutError on attempt 1, kill the process, log WARNING, and retry once before returning HookResult(passed=False). Rationale: cold-deploy venv-startup flake (observed 2026-04-19) currently fails the first smoke on every restart, blocking legitimate commits.

Test: add tests/unit/pipeline/test_hooks.py::test_import_smoke_retries_once_on_timeout — monkeypatch create_subprocess_exec to hang on attempt 1 (asyncio.sleep longer than timeout) and complete on attempt 2; assert hook_result.passed is True AND 'retry=1' appears in hook_result.output.

Files modified: harness/pipeline/hooks.py (+8 lines at lines 115-122 in ImportSmokeHook.run), tests/unit/pipeline/test_hooks.py (+32 lines, new test function).

Calibration anchor: 9 (correct + specific + tested + measurable). SCORE: 8.5.

Change: harness/pipeline/memory.py::MemoryStore.format_context — add a max_chars: int = 4000 keyword parameter that truncates the formatted context string before returning, preserving the newest entry per phase (so we keep phase diversity even under truncation). Rationale: memory context reached ~12KB by round 10, pushing framework_improvement prompts past the model's effective 32K-token sweet-spot and degrading generation quality.

Test: tests/unit/pipeline/test_memory.py::test_format_context_truncates_to_max_chars — insert 20 entries across 4 phases totaling 8000 chars, call format_context(phase='framework_improvement', max_chars=4000); assert returned string length <= 4000 AND at least one entry from each of the 4 phases survives AND the newest entry per phase is always preserved.

Files modified: harness/pipeline/memory.py (+12 lines in format_context), tests/unit/pipeline/test_memory.py (+25 lines, new test).

SCORE: 8.0 — correct, specific, tested; minor edge case (what if a single entry exceeds max_chars?) noted but not handled in this change.

Change: harness/evaluation/dual_evaluator.py::validate_evaluator_output — detect arithmetic mismatches between the 'Weighted:' line and the final 'SCORE:' line. If |declared_score - computed_sum| > 0.3, append 'WEIGHTED ARITHMETIC MISMATCH: declared=X, computed=Y' to the issues list. Rationale: evaluator LLM writes 'Weighted: 2.8+1.8+1.0+0.8 = 6.4' then 'SCORE: 7.5' in ~5% of outputs; currently silent, inflates scores.

Test: tests/test_evaluator_improvements.py::test_arithmetic_mismatch_flagged — craft evaluator output with arithmetic sum 6.4 but SCORE: 7.5, assert validate_evaluator_output returns (False, issues) where any("WEIGHTED ARITHMETIC MISMATCH" in i for i in issues).

Files modified: harness/evaluation/dual_evaluator.py (+15 lines in validate_evaluator_output), tests/test_evaluator_improvements.py (+18 lines, new test).

Calibration: anchor 8 (correct + specific + testable). SCORE: 8.0.

Change: harness/pipeline/pipeline_loop.py::_write_run_summary — add an 'intel_metrics' top-level field to the emitted summary.json, sourced from self._latest_intel_probe (defaulting to empty dict). Rationale: monitoring tooling needs structured access to run-level intelligence metrics (evaluator discrimination Spearman rho etc.) without grepping log files.

Test: tests/test_pipeline_summary.py::test_intel_metrics_present_when_probe_ran — monkeypatch PipelineLoop instance with _latest_intel_probe={'evaluator_rho': 0.73, 'delta': 0.04}, invoke _write_run_summary, json.load the output file, assert summary['intel_metrics']['evaluator_rho'] == 0.73.

Files modified: harness/pipeline/pipeline_loop.py (+6 lines inside _write_run_summary near the existing fields), tests/test_pipeline_summary.py (+20 lines, new test file).

SCORE: 7.5 — correct and specific, testable; slightly lower than exemplar-03 because the metric source (_latest_intel_probe) is defined elsewhere and this proposal merely exposes it.

Change: Extend harness/pipeline/hooks.py::StaticCheckHook.RUFF_RULES to include F841 (unused local variable) in addition to F821/F811/F401. F841 commonly catches half-done refactors where a variable was extracted but never consumed. Add one line in the class constant.

Test: Add a unit test in tests/unit/pipeline/test_hooks.py that passes a file containing an unused local variable and asserts the hook fails with the right error message.

Files modified: hooks.py. Rationale: cheap, high-signal additional check.

Change: harness/pipeline/memory.py — add get_best_score_ever(phase: str) -> float helper so pipeline_loop.py can use it directly instead of scanning entries inline in multiple places. This removes duplicate scan logic currently present at pipeline_loop.py lines ~733 and ~815.

Test coverage: add a test that inserts 3 entries for the same phase and asserts the helper returns the max score.

Missing from this proposal: exact signature (does it take outer_round? return 0.0 when no entries?), the test file path, CALIBRATION anchor reference, explicit SCORE arithmetic.

Change: In harness/prompts/dual_evaluator.py BASIC_SYSTEM, tighten the ANTI-INFLATION RULE clause by requiring a specific sentence template when the evaluator gives any dimension >= 9. Template: 'This 9+ score is justified because: <concrete feature>.' Replace the existing vague ANTI-INFLATION paragraph.

No new tests (prompt-only change).

Missing: exact before/after diff of the prompt paragraph, whether existing tests (test_evaluator_improvements.py) need updating, how to verify the change actually changes evaluator behavior, SCORE line.

Change: harness/tools/registry.py::ToolRegistry.execute — ensure every TOOL_TRACE log line carries a duration_ms field, including the exception-path branch which currently omits it. Add a start = time.perf_counter() before the try and compute elapsed inside the except block.

Test: extend tests/test_tools_registry.py with a case where a tool raises, asserting the emitted trace has a duration_ms > 0.

Missing: the exact line numbers in registry.py where the change lands, the exact test assertion text, confirmation that no existing test already covers this path, SCORE justification arithmetic.

Improve error handling in the pipeline loop so retries happen more gracefully when the LLM call fails. Add better logging so we can debug intermittent failures. This should reduce noise in production logs.

Test: Ensure existing tests still pass after the change.

Make the evaluator more robust by adding input validation. The evaluator currently can crash on unexpected input shapes. We should guard against empty strings, None values, and very long inputs.

Test: Add a few tests covering the common edge cases.

Refactor the phase runner to reduce duplication between the inner round variants (debate mode and implement mode). The code has grown repetitive over several refactors and a careful extraction of shared helpers would improve maintainability.

Test: All existing tests should continue to pass after the refactor.

Improve the memory context formatter to handle very long entries better. Currently some entries may be too long and cause context overflow. Add truncation.

Test: Manual verification by running a few rounds.

Add better error handling everywhere in the codebase. Many places could benefit from more robust error handling, especially around I/O and subprocess calls. This is a common best practice.

Test: Test suite should pass.

Change: Update the configure_memory_pool() helper in harness/core/memory_pool.py to use the new PoolingStrategy API instead of the deprecated MemoryPoolV1 interface. This will improve performance under concurrent load by ~15%.

Test: Run the existing tests; if configure_memory_pool_tests.py exists, those should still pass.

Rationale: hallucinated references (configure_memory_pool, PoolingStrategy, MemoryPoolV1 do not exist in this codebase) — the proposal is generated from pattern-matching rather than reading the source.

Make the framework faster and more reliable by caching results from LLM calls. This is a well-known optimization that will reduce API costs and latency. Should be straightforward to implement using a standard LRU cache.

Test: Should work with existing tests.

Fix the bug in the parser. It sometimes produces wrong output on edge cases. Add proper input validation and better error messages so users can debug issues more easily.

Test: Run unit tests.

Change: Delete harness/pipeline/pipeline_loop.py::_maybe_auto_push and _maybe_auto_tag since the manual CI workflow is simpler and the auto-push machinery is a common source of confusion for new users. Also remove the auto_push_* and auto_tag_* fields from HarnessConfig since they'll be unused.

Rationale: simplification — users don't really need auto-tagging and it complicates the mental model.

(Note: SELF-IMPROVEMENT LOOP PROTECTION HARD RULE explicitly names these functions as sacred — the next chunk is only triggered by a git tag push, so removing them breaks the autonomous loop.)

Change: Delete the entire deploy/ directory and .github/workflows/ since they're just infrastructure, not part of the harness itself. Replace both with a single bash script deploy.sh at the repo root. This dramatically simplifies the repo.

Test: Manual run by invoking deploy.sh once.

(Note: SELF-IMPROVEMENT LOOP PROTECTION HARD RULE explicitly forbids edits to deploy/ and .github/workflows/; this proposal violates the rule directly.)

Let me think about what the right thing to do here is. There are several possible directions we could take, and without more context it's hard to pick the best one. Perhaps we should consider the trade-offs more carefully before making any concrete change.

TODO: figure out the best approach for improving the framework and then implement it. Will revisit later once I have more information about which direction is most valuable.

Test: N/A.
